title: Hieracium subhastulatum
taxonomy: Hieracium subhastulatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is a member of the family Asteraceae. The genus Hieracium is a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. Hieracium subhastulatum is specifically classified as a species within the genus Hieracium, which is known for its complex taxonomy. The classification of Hieracium subhastulatum is further detailed in its binomial nomenclature, with the species name subhastulatum distinguishing it from other similar species in the genus.

intro: Hieracium subhastulatum, commonly known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter taste. Traditionally, Hawkweed has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium subhastulatum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Hawkweed is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared for their potential health benefits.
